text:
COMBINED GERMLINE AND SOMATIC MOLECULAR PROFILING REPORT

================================================================================
PATIENT INFORMATION
================================================================================
Patient Name: Marguerite Okonkwo
Date of Birth: [on file]
Sex: Female
Ordering Physician: [Gynecologic Oncology, attending on file]
Diagnosis: High-grade serous carcinoma, ovary (bilateral), FIGO Stage IIIC
Specimen Source (Somatic): Omental biopsy, obtained at time of primary debulking surgery
Specimen Source (Germline): Peripheral blood (EDTA)
Laboratory (Germline): Myriad Genetics, Inc. | Salt Lake City, UT | CLIA #: 46D0895445
Laboratory (Somatic): Foundation Medicine, Inc. | Cambridge, MA | CLIA #: 22D2027531
Test Platforms: Myriad myRisk Hereditary Cancer (35-gene panel); Foundation One CDx (324 genes, FDA-approved companion diagnostic)

================================================================================
SECTION I: GERMLINE GENETIC TESTING REPORT
Myriad myRisk Hereditary Cancer Panel - 35 Gene Panel
================================================================================

INDICATION FOR TESTING:
Hereditary cancer risk assessment in the setting of newly diagnosed high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, FIGO Stage IIIC. Personal history of ovarian malignancy. Family history notable for mother with ovarian cancer (deceased age 55) and maternal aunt with breast cancer (diagnosed age 42). West African (Nigerian-American) descent. BRCA1 protein expression loss noted on tumor immunohistochemistry (loss of nuclear BRCA1 staining in malignant cells with retained expression in stromal cells, on ascitic fluid cell block).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL SUMMARY - GERMLINE RESULTS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

FINDING 1 (PATHOGENIC - ACTIONABLE):
Gene: BRCA1
Variant: c.5266dupC (p.Gln1756ProfsTer74)
Classification: CLASS 5 - PATHOGENIC
Variant Type: Frameshift insertion (single cytosine duplication)
Historical Nomenclature: BRCA1 5382insC (legacy designation); also referenced in literature as "5382insC founder mutation"
Chromosomal Location: 17q21.31
Variant Allele Frequency (germline blood): 48.2% (consistent with germline heterozygosity; expected ~50% in diploid tissue)
zygosity: Heterozygous

Interpretation:
BRCA1 c.5266dupC (p.Gln1756ProfsTer74) is a well-characterized pathogenic frameshift variant resulting from the insertion of a single cytosine at nucleotide position 5266 within exon 20 of BRCA1. This insertion causes a translational frameshift leading to a premature termination codon 74 codons downstream (p.Gln1756ProfsTer74), predicting the production of a severely truncated, non-functional BRCA1 protein that is subject to nonsense-mediated mRNA decay. Loss of BRCA1 function eliminates its critical role in high-fidelity homologous recombination (HR) DNA repair, rendering cells reliant on error-prone repair pathways and conferring genomic instability.

This variant is also known historically as "5382insC" (numbered according to legacy cDNA reference sequences) and represents one of the most extensively studied BRCA1 founder mutations in the world. It is highly prevalent in individuals of Ashkenazi Jewish descent (carrier frequency approximately 1 in 40), individuals of Polish and Eastern European ancestry, and has also been identified in West African and other populations at lower frequencies. The variant has been reported in thousands of individuals and families with BRCA1-associated hereditary breast and ovarian cancer (HBOC) syndrome and is definitively classified as pathogenic per ACMG/AMP variant interpretation guidelines (supporting criteria: PVS1, PS1, PP5 - multiple lines of evidence).

Lifetime Cancer Risk Estimates Associated with BRCA1 Pathogenic Variant (heterozygous carrier):
  - Female breast cancer (lifetime to age 80): 72% (range 65-79%)
  - Ovarian cancer (lifetime to age 80): 44% (range 36-53%)
  - Contralateral breast cancer (after ipsilateral diagnosis): ~40% at 10 years
  - Pancreatic cancer: modestly elevated (~2-3x general population)
  - Prostate cancer (in male carriers): elevated risk (for family members)

Clinical Implications:
This pathogenic BRCA1 variant is the causative germline mutation underlying this patient's high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma. Patients with germline BRCA1/2 mutations and ovarian cancer are candidates for PARP inhibitor therapy, including both first-line maintenance (niraparib, olaparib) and treatment (rucaparib, olaparib) settings. Platinum sensitivity is also expected to be enhanced in BRCA1/2-mutated tumors. Cascade genetic testing is strongly recommended for first- and second-degree biological relatives.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINDING 2 (VARIANT OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE - NOT ACTIONABLE):
Gene: BRCA2
Variant: c.8167G>A (p.Asp2723Asn)
Classification: CLASS 3 - VARIANT OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS)
Variant Type: Missense substitution (aspartic acid to asparagine at amino acid 2723)
Chromosomal Location: 13q12.3
Variant Allele Frequency (germline blood): 47.6% (consistent with heterozygous germline)
Zygosity: Heterozygous

Interpretation:
BRCA2 c.8167G>A (p.Asp2723Asn) is a missense substitution within the DNA binding domain of BRCA2. This variant has been reported in the literature and in population databases at very low frequency. In silico predictive algorithms yield conflicting results (SIFT: deleterious; PolyPhen-2: possibly damaging; REVEL score: 0.42 - intermediate). Functional studies for this specific variant are limited. In the context of co-occurring BRCA1 pathogenic variant, this VUS is unlikely to be contributing additional independent oncologic risk, and it does not alter current clinical management. The variant has been submitted to ClinVar. Per ACMG/AMP variant classification criteria, insufficient evidence currently exists to classify this variant as pathogenic or benign.

This VUS is NOT clinically actionable at this time. Re-evaluation of this variant's classification will occur as new evidence becomes available in the literature, population databases, and functional studies. Periodic recontact with the ordering provider is recommended should reclassification occur.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
REMAINDER OF 35-GENE PANEL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
The following genes were analyzed and NO PATHOGENIC VARIANTS OR LIKELY PATHOGENIC VARIANTS WERE IDENTIFIED:
ATM, BARD1, BRIP1, CDH1, CHEK2, EPCAM, FAM175A (ABRAXAS1), MEN1, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, MUTYH, NBN, NF1 (germline), PALB2, PMS2, PTEN, RAD51C, RAD51D, RET, SMAD4, STK11, TP53 (germline Li-Fraumeni evaluation: NO pathogenic germline TP53 variant identified), VHL, and additional panel genes.

Variants of uncertain significance in other panel genes: NONE additionally detected.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GERMLINE TESTING METHODOLOGY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DNA was extracted from peripheral blood (EDTA tube). Sequencing was performed using next-generation sequencing (NGS) with a mean depth of coverage >500x across all targeted regions. Large rearrangement analysis (deletion/duplication) was performed using SNP-based copy number analysis (BART: BRCA Analysis Rearrangement Test). All reportable variants confirmed by orthogonal methodology (Sanger sequencing or ddPCR as applicable). Analytical sensitivity for small variants (SNVs and indels): >99%. Analytical sensitivity for large rearrangements: >99%. Variants classified per the American College of Medical Genetics and Genomics (ACMG)/Association for Molecular Pathology (AMP) guidelines (Richards et al., Genetics in Medicine, 2015) using an evidence-based framework incorporating population data, computational predictions, functional data, and co-segregation evidence.

================================================================================
SECTION II: SOMATIC TUMOR GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
Foundation One CDx (324-Gene Comprehensive Genomic Profile)
================================================================================

SPECIMEN DETAILS:
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue block
Tissue Source: Omental biopsy
Procedure Date: [on file - primary debulking surgery]
Tumor Purity (estimated by pathology review): ~65% (adequate for genomic analysis; minimum threshold met)
Tissue Adequacy: ADEQUATE - specimen passed all quality control metrics

DIAGNOSIS ON SUBMITTED SPECIMEN: High-grade serous carcinoma, omentum (metastatic)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TMB: 6.2 mutations/Mb
Interpretation: TMB-LOW (threshold for TMB-High: >=10 mutations/Mb per FDA-approved pembrolizumab indication)
Note: TMB-low is typical of high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma; this result is not unexpected for this histologic subtype. PD-L1 expression by IHC (companion diagnostic): CPS 5 (low; pembrolizumab not indicated for TMB-low/PD-L1-low ovarian carcinoma in this context).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSATELLITE STABILITY STATUS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Microsatellite Status: MSS (Microsatellite Stable)
Loci analyzed: 114 intronic homopolymer repeat loci
Interpretation: MSS. Not MSI-High. Lynch syndrome screening (somatic MMR deficiency) not indicated by this result, consistent with germline panel findings (no pathogenic MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, or EPCAM variants detected).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
HOMOLOGOUS RECOMBINATION DEFICIENCY (HRD) STATUS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
HRD Score (genomic scarring composite): 62
Threshold for HRD-Positive: >=42
HRD Status: HRD-POSITIVE

HRD Score Components:
  - Loss of Heterozygosity (LOH) Score: 24 (elevated; genome-wide LOH events)
  - Telomeric Allelic Imbalance (TAI) Score: 22 (elevated)
  - Large-Scale State Transitions (LST) Score: 16 (elevated)

Interpretation:
This tumor demonstrates a markedly elevated HRD score of 62 (well above the validated threshold of 42 for HRD-positivity, as defined by the Myriad myChoice CDx assay standard). HRD-positivity in the context of a germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant with confirmed somatic LOH at the BRCA1 locus (see below) is consistent with biallelic BRCA1 inactivation driving genomic instability and homologous recombination deficiency. This tumor is expected to be highly sensitive to platinum-based chemotherapy and PARP inhibitors.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SOMAIC GENOMIC ALTERATIONS - DETAILED FINDINGS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

** TIER I: ACTIONABLE / CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT ALTERATIONS **

1. TP53
   Alteration: Missense mutation - p.R175H (c.524G>A)
   Variant Allele Frequency: 68%
   Classification: PATHOGENIC - DRIVER MUTATION
   Tier: I (Well-established cancer driver)
   Notes: TP53 p.R175H is one of the most common and well-characterized "hotspot" gain-of-function TP53 mutations in human cancer. Located in the DNA binding domain of p53, this substitution (arginine to histidine at codon 175) abolishes wild-type p53 DNA binding activity and confers gain-of-function oncogenic properties (including dominant negative inhibition of remaining wild-type p53 alleles). The elevated VAF of 68% - above the expected ~50% for a simple heterozygous somatic mutation - is consistent with allelic imbalance or copy-neutral LOH at the TP53 locus (17p13.1), leading to enrichment of the mutant allele. The aberrant diffuse strong nuclear p53 immunostaining pattern observed on the cell block IHC is the classical immunophenotypic correlate of a missense gain-of-function TP53 hotspot mutation. TP53 mutation is a near-universal event in high-grade serous carcinoma (present in >96% of cases per TCGA HGSC data) and is the defining molecular hallmark of this histologic subtype. Not directly actionable with approved therapeutics; serves as diagnostic confirmatory finding.

2. BRCA1
   Alteration: Somatic Loss of Heterozygosity (LOH) at BRCA1 locus (17q21.31)
   LOH Detection: Genome-wide SNP array-based LOH analysis identifies loss of the wild-type BRCA1 allele
   VAF Context: Consistent with biallelic BRCA1 inactivation (germline pathogenic c.5266dupC + somatic LOH = complete loss of BRCA1 function in tumor)
   Classification: PATHOGENIC - DRIVER ALTERATION (second hit)
   Tier: I (Directly actionable; PARP inhibitor biomarker)
   Notes: BRCA1 somatic LOH, in the context of a germline heterozygous pathogenic BRCA1 c.5266dupC variant, confirms biallelic inactivation of BRCA1 in this tumor. This is the expected "second hit" mechanism in BRCA1-associated cancers, consistent with Knudson's two-hit hypothesis. Complete BRCA1 functional loss is the molecular basis for this tumor's HRD-positive phenotype, elevated genomic scarring score, and predicted sensitivity to platinum agents and PARP inhibitors.

   Companion Diagnostic Relevance:
   - OLAPARIB (Lynparza): FDA-approved for BRCA1/2-mutated advanced ovarian cancer (both treatment and first-line maintenance; BRCAm companion diagnostic)
   - NIRAPARIB (Zejula): FDA-approved for first-line maintenance in advanced ovarian cancer (HRD-positive biomarker; PRIMA/ENGOT-OV16 data; individualized starting dose per patient weight/platelet criteria)
   - RUCAPARIB (Rubraca): FDA-approved for BRCA1/2-mutated ovarian cancer treatment setting
   - PLATINUM CHEMOTHERAPY: Enhanced sensitivity expected in BRCA1/2-deficient tumors

** TIER II: BIOLOGICALLY RELEVANT ALTERATIONS (LIKELY DRIVER OR SIGNIFICANT COPY NUMBER EVENTS) **

3. RB1
   Alteration: Homozygous deletion (copy number: 0)
   Chromosomal Location: 13q14.2
   VAF: N/A (copy number event)
   Classification: LIKELY PATHOGENIC - TUMOR SUPPRESSOR LOSS
   Tier: II (Significant oncogenic event; no approved targeted therapy directly)
   Notes: Homozygous deletion of RB1, encoding the retinoblastoma protein (pRb), results in complete loss of G1/S cell cycle checkpoint function. RB1 loss is a recurrent event in HGSC (~17% of cases per TCGA) and is associated with CDK4/6 pathway dysregulation. In the context of concurrent CCNE1 amplification (see below), biallelic RB1 loss and CCNE1 amplification represent a convergent mechanism of cell cycle deregulation. RB1 loss is mutually exclusive with CDK4/6 inhibitor benefit in some tumor contexts (CDK4/6 inhibitors require intact Rb for mechanism of action), which may be relevant for future clinical trial eligibility.

4. CCNE1 (Cyclin E1)
   Alteration: Amplification
   Copy Number: 12 (high-level amplification; diploid reference 2)
   Chromosomal Location: 19q12
   VAF: N/A (copy number event)
   Classification: LIKELY PATHOGENIC - ONCOGENE AMPLIFICATION
   Tier: II (Clinically significant; no approved targeted therapy in this indication)
   Notes: CCNE1 amplification is observed in approximately 20% of HGSC (TCGA) and encodes Cyclin E1, a critical activator of CDK2 that promotes S-phase entry. High-level CCNE1 amplification (copy number 12) is one of the most significant oncogenic drivers of HGSC alongside TP53 mutation and BRCA1/2 deficiency. Importantly, CCNE1 amplification in HGSC is strongly associated with de novo resistance to PARP inhibitors and is considered an alternative pathway of cell cycle dysregulation distinct from - and partially antagonistic to - the HR deficiency pathway. Tumors with concurrent BRCA1 inactivation AND high CCNE1 amplification represent a molecularly complex subgroup; the CCNE1 amplification may serve as a clonal mechanism of partial PARP inhibitor resistance within this tumor, though the dominant clone appears HR-deficient based on HRD score. Clinical implications warrant close monitoring for early resistance signals. CCNE1-directed therapies (e.g., CDK2 inhibitors, such as investigational agents) are in early-phase clinical development.

5. CDK12
   Alteration: Amplification
   Copy Number: 6
   Chromosomal Location: 17q12
   VAF: N/A
   Classification: LIKELY PATHOGENIC - ONCOGENE AMPLIFICATION
   Tier: II
   Notes: CDK12 amplification is distinct from CDK12 biallelic inactivation (which characterizes a separate genomic subclass of prostate cancer). In ovarian cancer, CDK12 amplification co-occurs with ERBB2 amplification at 17q12 in some cases, though no ERBB2 amplification is detected here. CDK12 is a transcriptional cyclin-dependent kinase that regulates RNA polymerase II and DNA damage response gene expression. CDK12 amplification in this tumor may modestly upregulate DNA repair gene transcription, which could provide partial compensation for BRCA1-mediated HR deficiency. Not directly targeted by approved agents in ovarian cancer.

** TIER III: VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN OR SUBCLONAL SIGNIFICANCE **

6. PIK3CA
   Alteration: Missense mutation - p.E545K (c.1633G>A)
   Variant Allele Frequency: 14%
   Classification: PATHOGENIC (well-characterized activating mutation) - LIKELY SUBCLONAL
   Tier: III (Subclonal; likely not clonal driver in this tumor)
   Notes: PIK3CA p.E545K is a well-characterized hotspot activating mutation in the helical domain of the p110-alpha catalytic subunit of PI3-kinase, resulting in constitutive PI3K pathway activation (increased PIP3 generation, AKT/mTOR signaling). The low VAF of 14% suggests this mutation is subclonal (present in a minor tumor cell population), and it may represent secondary clonal evolution rather than a founding event. PIK3CA mutations occur in approximately 12% of HGSC. While PIK3CA p.E545K is a predictive biomarker for PI3K inhibitors (alpelisib, copanlisib) in breast cancer (with PIK3CA-mutated HR+ HER2- breast cancer), PI3K inhibitors are not currently FDA-approved for ovarian cancer. This finding may be relevant for future clinical trial eligibility.

7. NF1
   Alteration: Frameshift deletion - p.Q1846Lfs*17 (predicted truncating)
   Variant Allele Frequency: 22%
   Classification: LIKELY PATHOGENIC (truncating event in established tumor suppressor)
   Tier: III (No approved targeted therapy in this indication; RAS pathway context)
   Notes: NF1 encodes neurofibromin, a RAS-GAP (GTPase-activating protein) that negatively regulates RAS signaling by accelerating hydrolysis of RAS-GTP to RAS-GDP. Truncating NF1 mutations lead to loss of neurofibromin function and dysregulated RAS/MAPK pathway activation, functionally equivalent to an activating RAS mutation in some contexts. NF1 loss-of-function mutations occur in approximately 22% of HGSC (TCGA) and represent one of the mechanisms by which HGSC achieves RAS pathway activation in the absence of direct KRAS mutations (which are distinctly rare in HGSC, and none detected here). The intermediate VAF of 22% suggests this may represent a subclonal event or be present in a clonal population with copy number imbalance. MEK inhibitors have shown limited single-agent activity in NF1-altered solid tumors; this finding may be relevant for future clinical trials.

8. NOTCH3
   Alteration: Missense mutation - p.P1155S
   Variant Allele Frequency: 18%
   Classification: VARIANT OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS)
   Tier: III
   Notes: NOTCH3 p.P1155S is a missense change with uncertain oncogenic significance. NOTCH signaling is dysregulated in ovarian cancer, and NOTCH3 amplification has been reported, but this specific variant has insufficient functional data to establish pathogenicity. Not actionable at this time.

9. FAT1
   Alteration: Truncating mutation (frameshift; specific position on file)
   Variant Allele Frequency: 20%
   Classification: VARIANT OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS) - biologically plausible
   Tier: III
   Notes: FAT1 is a protocadherin with tumor suppressive activity via regulation of Wnt/beta-catenin signaling and Hippo pathway modulation. FAT1 truncating mutations are reported across multiple cancer types, but the specific clinical significance in HGSC is unclear. Not actionable at this time.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
NEGATIVE FINDINGS (KEY ABSENT ALTERATIONS - CLINICALLY INFORMATIVE)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
- KRAS: No mutation detected (KRAS mutations are characteristically absent in HGSC; their presence would suggest mucinous or low-grade serous histology, supporting histologic diagnosis)
- NRAS: No mutation detected
- BRAF: No mutation detected (V600E and all other BRAF mutations absent)
- ERBB2 (HER2): No amplification or activating mutation detected
- PTEN: No mutation or deletion detected (somatic; germline testing negative)
- ARID1A: No mutation detected (mutations more common in endometrioid and clear cell histologies)
- MLH1/MSH2/MSH6/PMS2: No somatic MMR gene mutations detected (consistent with MSS status)
- FGFR1/2/3: No amplification or activating mutations detected
- MET: No amplification or mutation detected
- ALK/ROS1/RET: No fusions or amplifications detected (not expected in ovarian HGSC)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMPREHENSIVE COPY NUMBER SUMMARY
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Genome-wide copy number analysis reveals a highly aneuploid tumor genome consistent with HGSC:
  - High-level amplifications (>6 copies): CCNE1 (19q12, CN=12), MYC (8q24, CN=8; clinically not specifically reportable as direct target, noted)
  - Focal amplifications (4-6 copies): CDK12 (17q12, CN=6), MECOM (3q26, CN=5)
  - Broad gains: Chromosome 3q, 8q, 20q
  - Homozygous deletions: RB1 (13q14.2, CN=0), BRCA1 region (17q21.31, LOH)
  - Broad losses: Chromosome 4q, 13q, 17p, 22q
  - Overall genomic instability: HIGH (consistent with HRD-positive, TP53-mutated HGSC)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PD-L1 EXPRESSION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PD-L1 IHC (22C3 pharmDx antibody, Dako):
  Combined Positive Score (CPS): 5
  Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 2%
  Interpretation: PD-L1 LOW. In the context of MSS, TMB-low high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, PD-L1 CPS 5 does not meet current thresholds for pembrolizumab (Keytruda) indication in ovarian cancer. Checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy is not indicated based on current regulatory approvals and available biomarker data.

================================================================================
SUMMARY OF ACTIONABLE AND CLINICALLY RELEVANT FINDINGS
================================================================================

PRIMARY ACTIONABLE ALTERATIONS:
1. BRCA1 germline pathogenic variant (c.5266dupC) + somatic LOH = Biallelic BRCA1 inactivation
   --> PARP INHIBITOR SENSITIVE (olaparib, niraparib, rucaparib); Enhanced platinum sensitivity
   --> FDA-approved companion diagnostic indication for PARP inhibitors confirmed
2. HRD Score = 62 (HRD-POSITIVE, above threshold 42)
   --> Supports PARP inhibitor sensitivity in first-line maintenance setting (niraparib per PRIMA/ENGOT-OV16)

BIOLOGICALLY RELEVANT / PROGNOSTIC:
3. TP53 p.R175H (68% VAF) - Defining HGSC driver; gain-of-function; confirms high-grade serous histology
4. CCNE1 amplification (CN=12) - Potential PARP inhibitor resistance biomarker; CDK2 pathway activation
5. RB1 homozygous deletion - Cell cycle dysregulation; potential CDK4/6 inhibitor resistance
6. NF1 frameshift (22% VAF) - RAS/MAPK pathway activation; subclonal
7. PIK3CA p.E545K (14% VAF) - PI3K pathway activation; subclonal

NOT ACTIONABLE / VUS:
8. CDK12 amplification (CN=6)
9. NOTCH3 p.P1155S (VUS)
10. FAT1 truncating mutation (VUS)

GERMLINE VUS (not clinically actionable):
11. BRCA2 c.8167G>A (p.Asp2723Asn) - VUS; periodic reclassification monitoring recommended

================================================================================
FAMILY IMPLICATIONS AND GENETIC COUNSELING RECOMMENDATIONS
================================================================================
Given the identification of a Class 5 Pathogenic Germline BRCA1 variant (c.5266dupC), genetic counseling is strongly recommended for all first-degree biological relatives:
  - Each first-degree relative (biological children, siblings, parents) has a 50% probability of carrying this variant
  - This patient's two biological children (one adult, one approaching adulthood) should be offered genetic counseling and BRCA1 testing when appropriate (typically at age 18 or when capable of informed decision-making)
  - Siblings of the patient should be offered testing
  - For female BRCA1 carriers: enhanced breast cancer surveillance (annual breast MRI + mammography beginning age 25-30), consideration of risk-reducing salpingo-oophorectomy (RRSO) by age 35-40 (or after completion of childbearing), and discussion of risk-reducing mastectomy
  - For male BRCA1 carriers: annual clinical breast exam, consideration of baseline prostate cancer screening; prostate cancer risk modestly elevated
  - The identified BRCA2 VUS (c.8167G>A) is NOT actionable for cascade testing at this time; family members need not be tested for this VUS until reclassification occurs

================================================================================
METHODOLOGY - SOMATIC (Foundation One CDx)
================================================================================
DNA was extracted from FFPE omental biopsy specimen. Library preparation and hybrid-capture based next-generation sequencing was performed targeting the full coding exons of 324 cancer-relevant genes, select introns of 36 genes frequently rearranged in cancer, and full coding exons of select RNA targets. Mean sequencing depth: >500x (median coverage >800x across all exons). Variant calling: SNVs (VAF >= 5%), indels (VAF >= 5%), copy number amplifications/deletions (>= 6 copies for amplification, <= 1 copy for deletion), gene rearrangements/fusions. Tumor mutational burden calculated from 1.1 Mb of sequenced genome. MSI status determined from 114 microsatellite loci. HRD analysis performed using genome-wide LOH, TAI, and LST scoring (validated algorithm). Results subject to germline filtering using matched normal methodology and population allele frequency databases (gnomAD, 1000 Genomes). Analytical sensitivity for somatic SNVs: >99% at VAF >= 5% with adequate tumor purity. FDA PMA approved as companion diagnostic for multiple PARP inhibitor and other targeted therapy indications (P170019).

Quality Control Metrics - Somatic Specimen:
  Tumor Purity: ~65% (pathology-verified)
  Mean Coverage: 847x
  Total Unique Reads Aligned: 214 million
  Uniformity (% bases >0.2 mean): 98.3%
  Specimen QC: PASS

================================================================================
REPORTING PATHOLOGIST / MOLECULAR GENETICIST
================================================================================
[Signed by: Molecular Pathology Attending, Foundation Medicine, Inc.]
[Co-signed by: Certified Genetic Counselor, Myriad Genetics, Inc.]

CAUTION: This report is intended for use by qualified healthcare professionals only. Results should be interpreted in the context of the patient's complete clinical, histopathologic, and family history. Variant classifications are subject to revision as new scientific evidence becomes available. Genetic counseling by a board-certified genetic counselor is strongly recommended for germline findings.

================================================================================
END OF REPORT
================================================================================
